name: 细胞信号
subject: biology
domain_name: Unit 3-4 细胞能量与通讯 Energy & Communication
curriculum_points:
BIG IDEA 1: EVOLUTION
The process of evolution drives the diversity and unity of life. Evolution is a change in the genetic makeup of a population over time, with natural selection as its major driving mechanism. Darwin’s theory, which is supported by evidence from many scientific disciplines, states that inheritable variations occur in individuals in a population. Due to competition for limited resources, individuals with more favorable genetic variations are more likely to survive and produce more offspring, thus passing traits to future generations.

LEARNING OBJECTIVE
7.2.A
Explain how natural selection affects populations.

ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE
7.2.A.1
Natural selection is a major mechanism of evolution. Natural selection acts on phenotypic variations in populations.

LEARNING OBJECTIVE
7.2.B
Explain how evolutionary fitness is affected by differential reproductive success.

ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE
7.2.B.1
Individuals with adaptations that are advantageous in an environment tend to have increased evolutionary fitness. These individuals are more likely to survive to reproductive age and produce offspring, thus passing traits to future generations.

LEARNING OBJECTIVE
7.2.C
Explain the relationship between changes in the environment and evolutionary changes in the population.

ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE
7.2.C.1
Evolution is also driven by random occurrences. Mutations and genetic drift can cause changes (evolution) in populations.

errors.description: 只记年代与名词，不能用史料与制度变迁解释历史进程。
把传说叙述与考古/文献证据混为一谈，未区分证据强度。

exercises.stem: 结合课标要求，说明「细胞信号」的核心内涵，并各举一条史料或史实依据。
exercises.answer:

exercises.stem:
用因果链解释：LEARNING OBJECTIVE
7.2.A
Explain how natural selection affects populations.

ESS… 对理解「细胞信号」为何重要？
exercises.answer: 需写出前因—制度/经济/思想变化—后果，避免只罗列年代。
